# Per-Hypothesis Context: H-M1

**Generated by:** Phase 2C Step 1 (JIT from 02b_verification_plan.md)
**Date:** 2026-05-04
**Hypothesis ID:** H-M1
**Type:** MECHANISM
**Gate:** MUST_WORK

---

## Hypothesis Info

**Statement:**
Under post-2018 OpenML tabular datasets with sufficient run history (≥ 10 runs, matched on creation year × task type × size), if F-UJI Findable sub-criteria score (persistent ID, metadata richness, indexed in search) is higher, then time-to-first-run will be significantly shorter (log-rank p < 0.05 on matched high/low Findable pairs; Findable Cox HR > 1.2), because persistent identifiers and rich metadata improve repository search ranking and dataset discoverability.

**Rationale:**
Causal Step 1 tests whether the Findable FAIR dimension specifically reduces discovery friction. This is a prerequisite for the full causal chain: if datasets cannot be found faster due to FAIR compliance, the mechanism is broken at the first link. Time-to-first-run is a clean discovery proxy that avoids download count noise.

**Success Criteria:**
- Primary: Log-rank p < 0.05; high-Findable median time-to-first-run < low-Findable median
- Secondary: Cox HR > 1.2 for Findable sub-criteria (20% faster discovery per unit increase)

**Failure Response:**
- IF log-rank p ≥ 0.05: EXPLORE — test Accessible sub-criteria as alternative discovery proxy
- IF sample size insufficient: SCOPE — relax matching criteria to 5:1 nearest-neighbor

---

## Experimental Setup

**Dataset:**
- Name: OpenML post-2018 tabular cohort (run timestamps)
- Type: programmatic-api
- Source: OpenML Python API (openml.org) — datasets + runs endpoints
- Path: Accessed programmatically via openml-python library
- Hypothesis Fit: OpenML provides structured run timestamp data per dataset, upload dates, and F-UJI Findable sub-scores. The ≥10 run filter ensures sufficient event history for survival analysis. Propensity matching on creation year × task type × size controls the major confounders identified in Phase 2A.

**Model (Analysis Method):**
- Name: Kaplan-Meier survival analysis + Cox proportional hazards regression
- Type: Statistical analysis (no ML model training)
- Source: lifelines library (Python); scipy; sklearn (propensity score matching); pandas
- Hypothesis Fit: Kaplan-Meier curves directly test whether high-Findable datasets experience shorter time-to-first-run. Cox PH regression estimates the Hazard Ratio for the Findable sub-criteria controlling for matched covariates. Log-rank test provides the primary p-value gate criterion.

---

## Variables

- **IV:** F-UJI Findable sub-criteria score (composite of F1_PID, F2_metadata, F3_search_indexed sub-metrics; 0.0–1.0)
- **DV:** Time-to-first-run (days from upload_date to first recorded experimental run on OpenML)
- **CV:** Creation year quartile, task type category, dataset size decile (matched via propensity scoring)

---

## Verification Protocol

1. From H-E1 cohort (5,000 scored datasets with FAIR scores), filter to datasets with ≥ 10 runs; extract per-dataset run timestamps via OpenML runs API.
2. Compute time-to-first-run per dataset (days from upload_date to earliest run timestamp).
3. Perform 1:1 propensity-score matching on creation year × task type × size; split into high/low Findable groups at median Findable sub-score.
4. Fit Kaplan-Meier survival curves for time-to-first-run in matched pairs; apply log-rank test (primary gate: p < 0.05).
5. Fit Cox proportional hazards regression with Findable sub-criteria as primary predictor; compute hazard ratio (secondary gate: HR > 1.2).
6. Sensitivity analysis: repeat with F-UJI ≥ 0.5 aggregate threshold instead of Findable median split.

---

## Baseline & Comparison Targets

| Method | Role | Dataset |
|--------|------|---------|
| Unadjusted KM curves (no propensity matching) | Baseline showing raw association with confounding | Full H-E1 cohort with ≥10 runs |
| F-UJI aggregate score vs. Findable sub-criteria | Test sub-criteria specificity vs. aggregate score | OpenML post-2018 cohort |
| Alternative IV: Accessible sub-criteria | Failure-response alternative discovery proxy | OpenML post-2018 cohort |

---

## Dependencies & Gate

- **Prerequisites:** H-E1 (VALIDATED ✅ — CV=0.1597, n_high=720, n_low=4280, bimodal confirmed)
- **Gate Type:** MUST_WORK
- **Gate Condition:** Log-rank p < 0.05 AND high-Findable median TFR < low-Findable median
- **Fail Action:** STOP downstream H-M2/H-M3; EXPLORE Accessible sub-criteria as alternative

---

## Previous Hypothesis Results (H-E1)

- CV=0.1597 (threshold 0.15) — marginal but clear pass
- n_high=720 (14.4% of 5000 datasets scored ≥ 0.5)
- n_low=4280 (85.6% scored < 0.5)
- Bimodal distribution confirmed (dip p=9.96e-6)
- Fallback proxy used (F-UJI API unavailable — proxy scoring applied)
- 29/29 unit tests passed

---

## Risk Factors

- **R1 (Medium):** Datasets with ≥10 runs may skew toward popular, high-FAIR datasets — selection bias; mitigated by propensity matching
- **R2 (High):** Run timestamps may be sparse for early post-2018 uploads — censoring; addressed via Kaplan-Meier right-censoring
- **R3 (Medium):** Findable sub-criteria may correlate with dataset age (older = more indexed) — confounding controlled by creation year matching
- **R4 (Low):** F-UJI API was unavailable for H-E1 (proxy used); same proxy will be reused for Findable sub-score extraction
